nts1.d: Part of the NTS family since day dot, Moxie has held her Wednesday show since the stations first broadcasts. Inviting esteemed guests for interviews & mixes and breaking new, unreleased music, she’s got your Wednesday afternoons covered… Tune in to hear anything from Soulful House, Garage, Afro beats and all the way through to rolling techno.

Regular journeys with Australia via Berlin DJ and producer Jennifer Loveless, channeling the dialogue between new and old dance music, oscillating between introspection and joy, with the occasional surprise and left turn.

nts1.n: Steven Julien Presents: The Apron Show

nts2.d: Prolific London based DIY & lo-fi pop producer Keel Her heads to the studio for a show once a month.

nts2.n: THE BADDIS SOUND W/ DANNIELLA DEE & E.T ROCKERS - DAD AND DAWTA!
nts2.d:
Inspired by the iconic reggae & ska sounds of her home, singer & producer Danniella Dee shares a monthly crate of dub-wise selections, soul-soothing rocksteady, and heavyweight roots to the studio.
From the host:_'Dee joins forces with her Dad E.T for the first time on NTS for a heartfelt h
our selection of 90's Roots, Dancehall and early Reggae.'

nts2.n: ARCHITECTS W/ BRONTË JANE: KIM DEAL SPECIAL (R)
THE WINDMILLS OF YOUR MIND w/ Taylor Rowley
SEA TO SEA w/ Diana Ratsamee
WHODIS? ALL FILIPINO MIX W/ THE HOMIE TIM
Rotting Fruit w/ Alfredo Travieso aka Secta Satanica

subtle.d: Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.
Potent Funk records’ Matt Frost - Well known for his technical abilities and Sleazy bass music style, Frost covers all angles from UK alt Rap to the LA beat scene.

Get ready to embark on a captivating sonic journey of underground music with Charlie Kelly. Including new and timeless sounds from the depths of rap, drill, grime, and experimental genres from the vibrant streets of the UK and beyond. Charlie unearths the very best in music that pushes boundaries. Off the mic, Charlie is a Radio Producer and Social Media Executive with an eclectic music taste spanning across many different cultures, decades, and styles. Hailing from North London with a love for audio and pop culture, you can expect to hear tune after tune when you’re locked into Charlie Kelly on Subtle.
BirdE brings an hour of the freshest Drum and Bass covering everything from liquid to techy steppers - the deeper the better.

Originally from Cambridge, Counter Culture's selection deftly navigates across a myriad of influences and exclusives from himself & friends.
Expect some of the freshest rolling soul straight to your headspace.

subtle.n: Strictly Business Show w/ Qua Rush & Flipz MC

subtle.d: In:flux Audio are a record label based in Leeds, UK founded by production duo Tik&Borrow. Since the label's inception in October 2013, they have dabbled in multiple dance music styles with the emphasis always being on the tracks being unique, and packed full of bass. Today the primary output for the label is from their Dubstep roster and this is focus of their show on Subtle - giving the artists and remixers chance to showcase their sounds with an hour guest mix, all hosted by label bossman Tik.

I’m a 19 year old DJ and producer from Newcastle upon Tyne, mainly playing and producing music around the 140bpm mark. I started out playing old 2-step and jungle records, before moving my focus to mainly dubstep and grime. Along with my friends Pirata and White Label, I run Newcastle’s only current 140 night, Well Irie.

subtle.n: THE CULTURE HEAD w. SAINT VOX
Skelecta presents: South London Dance Factory
In The Loop w/ Sam Groves

dublab.n: The Urxed - Seeing It As You Really Are
dublab.j: Seeing It As You Really Are

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: Hannah Rubin and Noelle Armstrong – MELLOW DRAMA
Frankie Jax No Mad - Not Evil Radio
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
